ddls:
CREATE TABLE "microcontrollers" (
  "microcontroller_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each microcontroller model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the microcontroller model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the microcontroller model */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the microcontroller */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the microcontroller model was released */,
  "specifications" TEXT /* Detailed specifications of the microcontroller model */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the microcontroller (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("microcontroller_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registers" (
  "register_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register */,
  "microcontroller_id" INTEGER /* ID of the microcontroller model the register belongs to */,
  "register_name" TEXT /* Name of the register (e.g., MODE1, SUBADR1) */,
  "register_type" TEXT /* Type of register (e.g., mode, subaddress) */,
  "function" TEXT /* Description of the register's function */,
  "read_write" TEXT /* Access permission for the register (e.g., read-only, read/write) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the register */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value the register can hold */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value the register can hold */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Indicates if the register is critical for the microcontroller's operation */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date the register was last modified */,
  "last_modified_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the register */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registers_microcontroller_id FOREIGN KEY ("microcontroller_id") REFERENCES microcontrollers ("microcontroller_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_registers_last_modified_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "register_configurations" (
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each register configuration */,
  "register_id" INTEGER /* ID of the register the configuration belongs to */,
  "decimal_value" INTEGER /* Decimal representation of the register value */,
  "hex_value" TEXT /* Hexadecimal representation of the register value */,
  "D7" INTEGER /* Bit 7 of the register value */,
  "D6" INTEGER /* Bit 6 of the register value */,
  "D5" INTEGER /* Bit 5 of the register value */,
  "D4" INTEGER /* Bit 4 of the register value */,
  "D3" INTEGER /* Bit 3 of the register value */,
  "D2" INTEGER /* Bit 2 of the register value */,
  "D1" INTEGER /* Bit 1 of the register value */,
  "D0" INTEGER /* Bit 0 of the register value */,
  "configuration_date" TEXT /* Date the configuration was created or last updated */,
  "configuration_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created or last updated the configuration */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the configuration is the default configuration */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments about the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("configuration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_register_configurations_register_id FOREIGN KEY ("register_id") REFERENCES registers ("register_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_register_configurations_configuration_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, administrator) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the configuration */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID of the access event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access (e.g., success, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_id") REFERENCES register_configurations ("configuration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the changes in the version */,
  "created_by_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "is_current" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is the current active version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_id") REFERENCES register_configurations ("configuration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_created_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("created_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "configuration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the configuration that triggered the notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who triggered the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message sent with the notification */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  "delivery_method" TEXT /* Method of delivery (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_configuration_id FOREIGN KEY ("configuration_id") REFERENCES register_configurations ("configuration_id")
)
